categories.name: Meta-qualities
categories.description: What makes someone hard to deal with, regardless of position

categories.name: Pressure
categories.description: Relaxed weight and control that shuts their movement down before you attack

categories.name: Connection
categories.description: Reading and controlling their center through the hips, shoulders, and limbs you hold

categories.name: Mind Games
categories.description: Forcing or predicting their next move and acting before they do

categories.name: Reputation & partners
categories.description: What people have told you — memories of events, not self-judgment

anchors.label: I don't know what to do here
I know the steps, haven't drilled much
Works in positional sparring vs less experienced
Works in free rolling vs same rank
Works vs bigger/higher rank — could teach it

scales.label: Works reliably against opponents up to
Training partners or coaches have told me
Where do you start to struggle?

questions.text: My top pins — Opponents conceding position before you attack
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; observable-event version of 'heavy pressure' — partner concedes without requiring an attack (Appendix B 'intense relaxed pressure' note); style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.problem: Opponents conceding position before you attack

questions.text: In my last 10 transitions between dominant positions, my partner recovered guard before I settled the new position
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; observable-event version of 'flow/connection' — partner interruption rate during transitions (Appendix B 'flow → partner doesn't recover guard mid-transition'); scored inverted — higher frequency is worse (flags: inverted)

questions.text: Their dominant position — Framing, breathing, and planning before they attack
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; composure under pressure — action-based (frame, breathe, plan) rather than felt quality; style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.problem: Framing, breathing, and planning before they attack

questions.text: A shifted base or broken grip — Entering before they reset
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; timing/anticipation — entry on moment of imbalance rather than general 'good timing'; style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.what: A shifted base or broken grip
slots.problem: Entering before they reset

questions.text: My first submission attempt — Their defense feeding your next attack
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; chaining — second attack enters from the shell of the first, observable from partner's reaction path; style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.problem: Their defense feeding your next attack

questions.text: Their submission setup — Stripping the grip or framing before they reach a finish
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; early-stage defense — pre-empt before danger zone (vs mid/late escape which is after the hold is set); style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.problem: Stripping the grip or framing before they reach a finish

questions.text: A near-finished submission — Creating space or changing the angle before tapping
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; late-stage escape — surviving a near-finish, distinct from early defense; style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.problem: Creating space or changing the angle before tapping

questions.text: An unfamiliar guard style — Finding a passing entry before the first minute ends
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; adaptability to unfamiliar guard — time-bounded observable outcome, not 'I can handle anything'; style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.problem: Finding a passing entry before the first minute ends

questions.text: A size or reach mismatch — Keeping your attack rate above zero through the first two rounds
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; adaptability to body type — attack-rate proxy for adaptation, concrete and measurable in memory; style-aligned to Gerald's 2026-07-20 pass

slots.problem: Keeping your attack rate above zero through the first two rounds

questions.text: Partners or coaches have told me my pressure makes them feel pinned before I start attacking
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; received-feedback version of pressure quality — external observer's report of the felt experience

questions.text: Training partners or coaches have asked me to show them a technique I just hit
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; skill-reputation item — being asked to teach is an observable social signal of perceived expertise

questions.text: Higher-ranked training partners have commented on a specific aspect of my game — pressure, guard, or finishing
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; skill-reputation item — unsolicited specific feedback from a higher rank is a memory of an event

questions.text: Partners at my level or above have sought me out specifically to roll, rather than me initiating
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; skill-reputation item — being sought out is an observable social signal, not self-judgment

questions.text: A coach or senior has given me specific advice about my game before a competition or grading
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; skill-reputation item — receiving targeted pre-competition input implies the coach sees something worth developing

questions.text: Partners or a coach have asked me to ease the intensity with a smaller or less-experienced training partner
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; partner-quality item (negatively valenced) — being asked to dial back is a concrete event that signals intensity mismatch; flagged for Gerald to review insight copy

questions.text: Newer students at the gym seek me out to roll because they feel they can learn from the round
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; partner-quality item (positively valenced) — beginners choosing you as a training partner signals perceived safety and generosity

questions.text: A coach has specifically paired me with a beginner or visitor and asked me to take care of them on the mat
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; partner-quality item — being trusted with beginners is a coach's observable vote of confidence in someone's mat conduct

questions.text: I have noticed that the same people decline to roll with me more than once, or step off the mat when I look for partners
questions.rationale: PLACEHOLDER — pending Gerald review; partner-quality item (negatively valenced) — being avoided is a pattern observable in memory; insight copy must be kind; flagged for Gerald
